overview: Based on the published agenda, the Village of Weston Board of Trustees was scheduled to hold a regular meeting to address multiple community matters including the Bull Junior View Subdivision plat approval and development agreement, discussion of SAFER operations following an April referendum, 2027 budget priorities, and capital projects including park renovations and street reconstruction.
committee: Board of Trustees, Village of Weston

discussions.item: Continuing Conversation with SAFER Following April Referendum Result
discussions.body: The Board was scheduled to discuss and consider action regarding ongoing matters related to the SAFER organization following the results of an April referendum. This discussion was expected to address organizational direction and potential decisions stemming from the referendum outcome.

discussions.item: Community Engagement Events Schedule and Activities
discussions.body: The Board was set to discuss and potentially take action on scheduling and planning of community engagement events and activities. This item was scheduled to review the Village's approach to community programming and event coordination.

discussions.item: Cintas Uniform Agreement
discussions.body: The Board was scheduled to discuss and consider action on a uniform services agreement with Cintas. This item was expected to address uniform procurement and services for Village staff.

discussions.item: Amendments to Chapter 5 Job Classification & Compensation and Chapter 10 Behavior & Conduct Personnel Policies
discussions.body: The Board was set to discuss and potentially take action on proposed amendments to the Village's personnel policies handbook, specifically provisions related to job classification, compensation, clothing allowances, and employee appearance standards.

discussions.item: Quarterly Update on Kennedy Park Renovation and Capital Campaign
discussions.body: The Board was scheduled for discussion only regarding progress on the Kennedy Park renovation project and related capital campaign fundraising efforts. This item was expected to provide an informational update on project status and fundraising activities.

discussions.item: Resolution 2026-019: Final Plat of Bull Junior View Subdivision
discussions.body: The Board was set to consider approval of the final plat for the Bull Junior View Subdivision, a development project involving Reedy Builders and Vreeland. This action was scheduled to formalize the subdivision's final design and boundaries.

discussions.item: President's Appointments to Committees and/or Commissions
discussions.body: The Board was scheduled to discuss and consider action on appointments by the President to various Village committees and commissions. This item was expected to address staffing of governance bodies that oversee Village operations.

discussions.item: D.C. Everest Area School District Facility Planning Presentation
discussions.body: The Board was set for discussion only regarding a presentation by D.C. Everest Area School District Superintendent Dr. Casey Nye and Assistant Superintendent Dr. Kelley Strike on the district's long-term facility planning project. This presentation was expected to inform the Board of school district capital planning matters.

discussions.item: Development Agreement for Bull Junior View Subdivision
discussions.body: The Board was scheduled to discuss and take action on a development agreement related to the Bull Junior View Subdivision. This was expected to formalize the terms and conditions for development of this residential project.

discussions.item: Mountain Bay Metropolitan Police Department Usage of Outbuildings at Public Safety Building Site
discussions.body: The Board was set to discuss and consider action on proposed usage of outbuildings at the public safety facility by the Mountain Bay Metropolitan Police Department. This item was scheduled to address space sharing and operational arrangements for the police department.

discussions.item: 2027 Budget Priorities and Budget Adoption Schedule
discussions.body: The Board was scheduled to discuss and consider action on establishing budget priorities for fiscal year 2027 and determining the schedule for budget adoption. This item was expected to set the direction for the upcoming annual budgeting process.

discussions.item: Need to Review Legal Detail as Referred by Finance and Human Resources Committee
discussions.body: The Board was set to discuss and consider action on a legal matter referred by the Finance and Human Resources Committee. This item was expected to address unspecified legal details requiring Board attention and decision-making.

discussions.item: 2025 Final Audit Report and letters
discussions.body: The Board was scheduled to discuss and consider action on the 2025 final audit report and related correspondence. This item was expected to review audit findings and recommendations for the completed fiscal year.

discussions.item: 2026 Audit Contract
discussions.body: The Board was set to discuss and consider action on contracting for the 2026 audit services. This item was scheduled to award or approve an audit services contract for the current fiscal year.

discussions.item: Installation of Food Allergy Signs in the Parks
discussions.body: The Board was scheduled to discuss and consider action on installing food allergy warning signs in Village parks. This item was expected to address public health safety measures at park facilities.

discussions.item: Howland Avenue Survey Services
discussions.body: The Board was set to discuss and consider action regarding survey services for Howland Avenue. This item was expected to address preliminary engineering or planning work for this street.

discussions.item: Concord Ave Reconstruction Project Bids
discussions.body: The Board was scheduled to discuss and consider action on bids received for the Concord Avenue reconstruction project. This item was expected to review contractor proposals for street reconstruction work.

discussions.item: Aquatic Center Leak Detection Contract
discussions.body: The Board was set to discuss and consider action on contracting for leak detection services at the Village's aquatic center. This item was scheduled to address facility maintenance and repair needs.

discussions.item: Mountain Bay Metro Police Department Bid Results
discussions.body: The Board was scheduled to discuss and consider action on bid results related to the Mountain Bay Metropolitan Police Department. This item was expected to review contractor proposals for police department needs or services.

discussions.item: Appraisals and Right-of-Way Purchases for Ross Avenue Reconstruction Project
discussions.body: The Board was scheduled to consider appraisals and right-of-way acquisitions for the Ross Avenue reconstruction project between Metro Drive and Alderson Street. This item was set for closed session discussion due to negotiation and purchasing considerations before possible action in open session.

publicComment: Public comment was scheduled for any person wishing to address the Board for up to three minutes regarding a non-agenda item, with time extension permitted at the Chief Presiding Officer's discretion.
